text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Neil.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目,我是Neil。

text: And I'm Georgie. In winter, people find different ways of keeping warm, like wearing extra clothes or staying indoors.
zh: 我是Georgie。冬天,人们会用各种方式保暖,比如多穿衣服或待在室内。

text: But in some countries, there's another way of keeping the cold out – going for a sauna.
zh: 但在一些国家,还有另一种驱寒方式——去蒸桑拿。

text: Yes. Nordic countries like Sweden and Finland have a long history of taking saunas, but now they're popping up in Britain too.
zh: 没错。像瑞典、芬兰这样的北欧国家有着悠久的桑拿传统,而现在英国也开始流行起来了。

text: Have you ever been for a sauna, Georgie? I have, yes, and if I'm honest, I'm not a huge fan.
zh: Georgie,你蒸过桑拿吗?蒸过,不过说实话,我不太喜欢。

text: I don't really like being too hot. It's uncomfortable. What about you, Neil?
zh: 我不太喜欢太热的感觉,觉得不舒服。Neil,你呢?

text: Oh, really? I quite like a sauna. You feel really refreshed afterwards.
zh: 哦,真的吗?我还挺喜欢桑拿的,蒸完之后感觉特别清爽。

text: So, what is a sauna? Well, a typical sauna is a small wooden room heated with steam to around 80 degrees Celsius.
zh: 那么,什么是桑拿呢?典型的桑拿房是一个用蒸汽加热到大约80摄氏度的小木屋。

text: It's hot enough to make anyone sweat. But are saunas good for your health as well?
zh: 这温度足以让任何人大汗淋漓。但桑拿对健康也有好处吗?

text: that's what James Gallagher wanted to find out for BBC Radio 4 programme, Inside Health. We're seeing what saunas do to the human body.
zh: 这正是James Gallagher想为BBC Radio 4的节目《Inside Health》探究的问题。我们要看看桑拿对人体究竟有什么作用。

text: They're popping up all over the country with claims that high temperatures boost your health and wellbeing.
zh: 桑拿房在全国各地如雨后春笋般涌现,有人声称高温能提升健康和身心状态。

text: But are they all they're cracked up to be? James wants to find out if saunas are all they're cracked up to be.
zh: 但它们真的名副其实吗?James想弄清楚桑拿是否真如人们所说的那样好。

text: He means, are they as good as people say? And that's exactly what we'll be finding out in this episode, along with some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 他的意思是,桑拿是否真的像大家说的那么好?这正是本期节目要探讨的内容,同时还会学到一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember, you can practice all the new vocabulary from this episode with the quiz and worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以通过我们网站bbclearningenglish.com上的测验和练习题,巩固本期学到的词汇。

text: But now I have a question for you, Georgie. The word sauna comes from the Finnish language, but what does it mean?
zh: 现在我有个问题要问你,Georgie。sauna这个词来自芬兰语,你知道它是什么意思吗?

text: Does it mean a sweat, b hot water or c bathhouse?
zh: 是a. 出汗,b. 热水,还是c. 浴室?

text: I'm going to guess c bathhouse. OK, well we'll find out if you're right at the end of the programme.
zh: 我猜是c,浴室。好,我们节目最后会揭晓答案。

text: In recent winters, saunas have grown in popularity in the UK.
zh: 最近几个冬天,桑拿在英国越来越流行。

text: BBC reporter James Gallagher met some people going to Moor a Sauna, a beach sauna in South Wales.
zh: BBC记者James Gallagher在南威尔士一家名为Moor a Sauna的海滩桑拿馆采访了一些常客。

text: He asked them why they love saunas so much. Hello everyone.
zh: 他问他们为什么这么喜欢桑拿。大家好。

text: Hello. Who loves a sauna? Me.
zh: 你好。谁喜欢桑拿?我。

text: Tell me why. Oh, it's just so relaxing.
zh: 说说原因吧。哦,真的很放松。

text: It just makes you feel at ease. It's also great for relaxing muscles if you're up tense or anything like that at all.
zh: 它让人感觉很自在,如果身体紧张或者哪里不舒服,桑拿对放松肌肉也很有帮助。

text: Yeah, I always love coming down here when I'm a bit stressed out.
zh: 是啊,每次压力大的时候我都爱来这儿。

text: And by the time that I'm finished, I feel like I've completely reset.
zh: 蒸完之后,感觉整个人像重新启动了一样。

text: It's wonderful. The first speaker says saunas make her feel at ease, a phrase meaning comfortable and relaxed.
zh: 真是太棒了。第一位受访者说桑拿让她感到‘at ease’,意思是感觉舒适、放松。

text: And the next speaker thinks saunas are great if you feel tense, an adjective meaning worried and unable to relax.
zh: 下一位受访者认为,如果你感到‘tense’——也就是紧张、无法放松——桑拿会很有帮助。

text: Saunas make the final speaker feel like she's reset.
zh: 桑拿让最后一位受访者感觉自己‘reset’了。

text: You might already know the word reset to mean turning a computer off and on again when it's not working,
zh: 你可能已经知道‘reset’这个词,指把电脑关机再开机以解决故障。

text: but used in connection with a person, reset means to start again after a rest so that you feel better about life.
zh: 但用在人身上时,‘reset’指经过休息后重新开始,让自己对生活感觉更好。

text: So, it seems saunas do help people feel better, but is there actual medical evidence that it's good for us?
zh: 看来桑拿确实能让人感觉更好,但真的有医学证据证明它对我们有益吗?

text: Here's James Gallagher again speaking with Professor Damian Bailey,
zh: 接下来还是James Gallagher,他采访了Damian Bailey教授,

text: an expert in human physiology for BBC Radio 4 programme Inside Health.
zh: 一位人体生理学专家,同样是为BBC Radio 4的《Inside Health》节目。

text: When we start to connect this to the arguments around health benefits, is there a health benefit to going in a sauna?
zh: 当我们把这个和健康益处的讨论联系起来时,蒸桑拿真的对健康有好处吗?

text: Yeah. I mean, I looked at the research really closely.
zh: 是的。我仔细研究过相关的研究。

text: I was fascinated by the evidence. huge benefits, I mean really huge benefits.
zh: 那些证据让我着迷——真的有巨大的益处,我是说真的非常大的益处。

text: And it's cultural of course, 5 million people in Finland, 90 % of them use saunas.
zh: 当然这也和文化有关,芬兰有五百万人口,其中90%的人都会蒸桑拿。

text: Maybe we should be copying what the Finns do, because there's a 40 % reduction
zh: 或许我们应该效仿芬兰人的做法,因为蒸桑拿的人全因死亡率降低了40%。

text: in all -cause mortality with the sauna goers.
zh: ……全因死亡率在蒸桑拿的人群中降低了40%。

text: Now this is what we would call observational data, but it's based on thousands and thousands of sauna goers.
zh: 这属于我们所说的观察性数据,但它基于成千上万蒸桑拿者的数据。

text: These are observational data sets, so we really do need what we call RCTs,
zh: 这些是观察性数据集,所以我们确实还需要所谓的RCT,

text: randomized controlled trials, so everything, just as you've mentioned there, everything is absolutely controlled.
zh: 也就是随机对照试验,正如你提到的那样,让一切都得到严格的控制。

text: But we can't turn our noses up at the current data, the observational data.
zh: 但我们也不能对现有的观察性数据嗤之以鼻。

text: Damian discusses the evidence from Finland, a country where 90 % of the population are regular sauna -goers.
zh: Damian谈到了来自芬兰的证据——在这个国家,90%的人经常蒸桑拿。

text: And it's impressive – a 40 % reduction in deaths from all causes.
zh: 这一数据令人印象深刻——全因死亡率降低了40%。

text: But it's important to remember that this is based on observational data –
zh: 但要记住,这只是基于观察性数据——

text: information collected from watching people's natural behaviour and not from controlled medical trials.
zh: 也就是通过观察人们的自然行为收集的信息,而不是通过对照医学试验得出的。

text: Nevertheless, this observational data is based on evidence from thousands and thousands of Finns over many years.
zh: 尽管如此,这些观察性数据来自多年间成千上万芬兰人的真实情况。

text: That's why Damien thinks we shouldn't turn our noses up at it.
zh: 这也是为什么Damian认为我们不应该对它嗤之以鼻。

text: We shouldn't reject it simply because we think it's not good enough for us.
zh: 也就是说,不能仅仅因为觉得它‘不够好’就直接否定它。

text: Well, all this talk about saunas has made me want to give it another try.
zh: 听了这么多关于桑拿的讨论,我都想再去试一次了。

text: But not before you reveal the answer to your question, Neil. I asked you what the Finnish word sauna means.
zh: 不过在那之前,Neil,先揭晓你问题的答案吧。我问的是芬兰语单词sauna是什么意思。

text: Is it a sweat, b hot water or c bathhouse?
zh: 是a. 出汗,b. 热水,还是c. 浴室?

text: And I said bathhouse, am I right? You are absolutely correct.
zh: 我说的是浴室,我说对了吗?你完全正确。

text: Well done. Yay! OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt in this episode.
zh: 太棒了!好,让我们来回顾一下本期学到的词汇。

text: If something is not all it's cracked up to be, it isn't as good as people say.
zh: 如果某样东西‘not all it's cracked up to be’,意思是它并没有人们说的那么好。

text: When someone's at ease, they feel comfortable and unstressed, whereas a tense person is worried and unable to relax.
zh: 当一个人‘at ease’时,表示他感觉舒适、没有压力;而‘tense’则表示焦虑、无法放松。

text: When someone resets, they start again after a rest, which has made them feel better about life.
zh: 当一个人‘reset’,意味着经过休息后重新开始,让自己对生活的感觉变得更好。

text: Observational data is information collected from watching people's natural behaviour without interfering in it.
zh: ‘Observational data’(观察性数据)指的是通过观察人们的自然行为、而不加干预所收集到的信息。

text: And finally, if you turn your nose up at something, you reject it because you think it's not good enough for you. Once again, our six minutes are up,
zh: 最后,如果你‘turn your nose up at’某样东西,就表示你因为觉得它配不上自己而拒绝它。好了,我们的六分钟又到了,

text: but if you want to improve your English with more trending topics and useful vocabulary, you'll find plenty on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 如果你想通过更多热门话题和实用词汇提升英语水平,欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,现在先说再见啦。
